stories.headline: British couple return to village at heart of deadly Spanish wildfire

stories.summary: Iran rejects any US control over strait as both sides trade attacks in major threat to negotiations to end their war.

stories.headline: British couple killed in Spanish wildfires as authorities race to identify victims
stories.summary: Appeal for DNA to help identify bodies as local government leader says ‘we are in a state of climate chaos’A British couple have been named as two victims of the wildfires in Spain that have killed 13 people, as authorities race to use DNA to identify those who did not manage to escape the blaze.Pete and Fran Gillam, who lived in Bédar, the village that bore the brunt of the wildfires on Thursday, are believed not to have survived. stories.headline: Sudan's paramilitary RSF chief sentenced to death over war crimes
stories.summary: Mohamed Hamdan Dagalo was convicted in absentia along with 15 RSF commanders and allies.

stories.headline: Trump reimposes US blockade and demands 20% Hormuz shipping fee
stories.summary: Trump declared the US would collect a 20% fee on all Hormuz cargo as "Guardian of the Strait," flipping the script on Iran's own toll demands, while US forces struck dozens of targets in Iran and the IRGC hit Bahrain, Jordan, Kuwait and Oman in the biggest exchange since the April ceasefire.

stories.headline: Leaders arrive for Ukraine talks in Paris as Macron hosts ‘coalition of the willing’ – Europe live
stories.summary: French president hosts leaders in Paris amid hopes that Ukraine’s recent advances could force Putin towards negotiationsin KyivMeanwhile, Russia has been forced to suspend shipping in the Sea of Azov after 90 vessels were targeted by Ukrainian drones in less than a week. stories.headline: Trump Says US Will Be Reimbursed 20% Rate for Hormuz Traffic
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said the US would reinstate its blockade of Iranian ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz and demanded a 20% reimbursement on all other cargo shipped through the waterway.

stories.headline: US and Iran trade fire as tensions rise over Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Within hours of the latest US attacks, Iran said it had struck US military assets in Kuwait, Jordan, Bahrain and Oman.

stories.headline: Trump recommends that Lindsey Graham’s sister serve rest of his Senate term
stories.summary: The president recommended that South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster select Darline Graham Nordone to serve into January. Her brother died suddenly amid a campaign for a fifth term.

stories.headline: Trump: US will ‘take over’ Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: President Donald Trump has said the US will ‘take over’ the Strait of Hormuz, accusing Iran of breaking the deal.

stories.headline: Hamas denies UN accusations over Gaza aid distribution interference
stories.summary: UN accuses Hamas of obstructing aid and risking workers' safety, but the group denies it and defends its actions.

stories.headline: Weather tracker: Unusually warm rivers affect French nuclear power plants
stories.summary: High temperatures and below average rainfall put pressure on waterways used to cool reactorsAbove average temperatures combined with below average rainfall across much of western and central Europe during June and the first half of July have placed increasing pressure on rivers, ecosystems and energy infrastructure. stories.headline: The late Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani’s legacy on women’s reform
stories.summary: Qatar's late Emir Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani’s 18-year rule brought pivotal changes towards women empowerment.

stories.headline: March to July: What’s different as US-Iran fighting escalates again?
stories.summary: With ceasefire in shambles and both sides exchanging tit-for-tat attacks, is the region again plunging into all-out war?

stories.headline: EU sanctions Russia's VK Company for helping expose Putin's critics
stories.summary: The EU has sanctioned VK Company for aiding the repression of critics of President Vladimir Putin and his war against Ukraine. The company says its services remain "available to users as normal".

stories.headline: Spanish PM Pedro Sánchez urges better prevention after deadly wildfire in south
stories.summary: The Prime Minister visited the southern municipality of Turre to meet the emergency services as they work to extinguish the fire.

stories.headline: How Lindsey Graham’s Journey From Trump Critic to Ally Made Him a Power Player
stories.summary: He initially called Donald Trump a “kook” who was “unfit for office,” only to join the new president’s Mar-a-Lago circle. “I’m still in the game,” Mr. Graham once explained of his much-analyzed turnabout.

stories.headline: Trump Backs Lindsey Graham’s Sister to Finish His Senate Term in South Carolina
stories.summary: Gov. Henry McMaster of South Carolina will name a replacement for Senator Lindsey Graham, who died on Saturday.

stories.headline: Lindsey Graham’s Sister Darline Nordone Was His Family
stories.summary: After the senator and his sister, Darline, lost their parents, he guided her through the teenage years and into college. He called her success the highlight of his life.

stories.headline: What We Know About Lindsey Graham’s Death
stories.summary: Senator Lindsey Graham died on Saturday night, a day after returning from Ukraine and four months before he was to face re-election.

stories.headline: What will happen to Trump’s agenda after Lindsey Graham’s death?
stories.summary: Graham, an ex-Trump critic, had turned into staunch supporter of the US president’s domestic and international policies.

stories.headline: Iran Shipped 57 Million Barrels of Crude Between US Blockades
stories.summary: Iran managed to export at least 57 million barrels of crude during a brief gap between two American naval blockades, highlighting what’s at stake for the global oil market now that restrictions are being reimposed.

stories.headline: Connecticut Towns Face Water-Fee Hikes as Bonds Finance Buyout

stories.headline: European Stocks Steady as Energy Offsets Travel, Tech Losses
stories.summary: European stocks were muted as declines in tech and travel shares were offset by gains in the energy sector, which was lifted by higher oil prices.

stories.headline: UBS Sees Goldman, JPMorgan in Focus on Busy Earnings Day
stories.summary: UBS Group AG analyst Erika Najarian says Goldman Sachs Group Inc. may have the tallest order when it comes to demonstrating earnings prowess this week, while investors may be focused on more on succession at JPMorgan Chase & Co. She previews Wall Street bank earnings on "Bloomberg Surveillance." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Mercurial and Magnetic, Lindsey Graham Was a Force in the Senate

stories.summary: The US president lashes out at fellow Nato member Spain, repeating an earlier threat to cut off trade relations.
